pick.rationale: Alabama enters with a massive analytical advantage at 16-17th in both KenPom and Torvik compared to Hofstra's 87-92nd range, while boasting the nation's 3rd-ranked offense that should overwhelm a CAA opponent. The Crimson Tide played the 3rd-toughest schedule nationally and went 13-5 in SEC play, while Hofstra's 196th-ranked schedule strength means they haven't faced anything close to this caliber of opponent. The major concern is the suspension of second-leading scorer Aden Holloway following his felony drug arrest, which creates some uncertainty, but Alabama's 70-spot analytical edge and elite offensive firepower provide enough cushion to absorb the loss. Hofstra's solid defense (19th in points allowed) and 25-year NCAA tournament drought-ending momentum give them a credible path to keep it competitive, but the talent gap between SEC and CAA competition makes this a relatively safe 4/13 matchup despite the off-court distraction.
